settlement.rules: Per Kalshi KXNEXTAG rules_primary: the market resolves YES for candidate X if X is the first NEW person to be Attorney General before Jan 20, 2029 15:00Z. Resolution covers the next confirmed AG after the current vacancy/acting period.
settlement.actingInterim: Verbatim from KXNEXTAG rules_secondary: 'Acting and Interim holders of the office are not included in the Payout Criterion. Persons confirmed by the Senate to be that position, or appointed via a "recess appointment" are included, even if that recess appointment is later struck down as illegal.'
ambiguities: 'First new person' is undefined relative to the prior incumbent — unclear if re-nomination/re-confirmation of the same person would count as 'new'.
Recess appointments count even if later struck down as illegal, but rules don't specify trigger timing (immediately on appointment, or only after some period).
No explicit handling of a nominee who is confirmed but never sworn in, dies, or withdraws before assuming office.
Deadline is Jan 20, 2029 15:00Z; ambiguous what happens if a confirmation vote occurs but swearing-in slips past that timestamp.
Only listed candidates have YES markets — if a person not on the candidate roster becomes AG, none of the YES contracts resolve YES.

facts.claim: DOJ Office of the Attorney General website lists Todd Blanche as Acting Attorney General; no Senate-confirmed AG currently in place.
Trump announced on Truth Social that Pam Bondi is out as AG and Deputy AG Todd Blanche will serve as Acting Attorney General.
AP confirms Trump's announcement firing Bondi and naming Blanche acting AG via Truth Social post.
CNN reports Trump fired Pam Bondi; Deputy AG Todd Blanche to serve as acting attorney general pending a permanent nominee.

Sen. Thom Tillis (R-NC, Judiciary): 'The threshold for somebody following Pam Bondi ends the moment I hear they said one thing that excused the events of January the 6th,' signaling he would block any AG nominee who excused Jan. 6.

Names publicly floated as contenders include Acting AG Todd Blanche, EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin, Harmeet Dhillon, and Jeanine Pirro; the White House declined to discuss contenders beyond Trump's Truth Social post.
Under the Federal Vacancies Reform Act, Blanche may serve as acting AG for up to 210 days from Bondi's departure (~late October 2026), creating deadline pressure on Trump's permanent nomination.
Acting AG Todd Blanche, asked at a DOJ press conference about staying on permanently, said any decision was up to President Trump.
Politico reports fierce internal competition is underway to be Trump's next AG nominee, with no permanent pick yet announced.
Politico: Sen. Thom Tillis would block any AG nominee on the Senate Judiciary Committee who excuses the Jan. 6 insurrection.

As of mid-May 2026, no permanent AG nominee has been formally submitted by the White House to the Senate; Blanche continues in acting capacity and is widely described as auditioning for the permanent role.

narratives.claim: Todd Blanche is Acting AG and leading prediction-market favorite for permanent AG
Pam Bondi was removed as AG ~April 2, 2026 over slow progress on Epstein files / political accountability
Sidney Powell is the frontrunner / Trump 'seriously considering' her for AG
Grassroots #SidneyPowellAG campaign urging Trump via Truth Social
Alina Habba floated as potential AG pick
Ron DeSantis mentioned in passing as possible AG
Kalshi market 'no AG announced by June 30' rose to ~53%, signaling expected delay
Trump publicly praised Blanche's performance as Acting AG
Critics label Blanche too moderate / 'Obama-era holdover'
Bondi transitioning to private sector post-removal

marketStructure.movement: Blanche slid sharply from ~54¢ to 44¢ (-10¢). Zeldin ticked up 25¢→28¢. Pirro nearly doubled 2.6¢→4.3¢. Longer-tail names weakened: DeSantis 3.7¢→2.1¢, Harmeet Dhillon 3.1¢→1.4¢. Ken Paxton popped 0.1¢→1¢ on small flow. Open interest heavy across the board (Blanche 392k, Pirro 354k, Zeldin 336k contracts), indicating an active, repricing market rather than a stale book.
marketStructure.limitations: Long-dated, 22-way mutually exclusive winner-take-all market resolving by Jan 20, 2029, with no acting/interim holders counted. Prices reflect narrative shocks (rumors, Trump statements, Senate-confirmation signals) more than a frequentist forecast. Books are wide (Pirro 2.9/4.2, Ed Martin 0.6/2.3) so marks overstate edge; mid vs last can differ 1-2¢. Public feed returns no 24h volume — cannot distinguish a single large print from sustained flow. Favorite at 44¢ implies 56% chance someone else; dispersion across 5+ live names means small fundamental news can re-rank the board quickly.

candidates.name: Todd Blanche
strengths: (INFER) Deep personal loyalty to Trump as his lead criminal defense attorney
(INFER) Already inside DOJ as Deputy AG → seamless succession optics
(INFER) Former federal prosecutor (SDNY) provides traditional DOJ credibility
(INFER) Already Senate-confirmed once (as DAG), reducing vetting friction
weaknesses: (INFER) Trump's personal-lawyer status raises independence/impartiality concerns
(INFER) Lacks independent political base outside Trump orbit
(INFER) MAGA populist wing may see him as too establishment
(INFER) Democratic unified opposition likely citing recusal conflicts
obstacles: (INFER) Senate math: needs near-unanimous GOP with ~zero Dem votes
(INFER) Recusal questions on any matter touching prior Trump representation
(INFER) Optics of elevating Trump's personal defense lawyer to top law-enforcement post
(INFER) Confirmation hearings would relitigate Trump prosecutions

candidates.name: Lee Zeldin
strengths: (INFER) Already Senate-confirmed (EPA) — proven confirmability
(INFER) Strong Trump loyalty (2020 election defense, NY gov run)
(INFER) Former House member with Hill relationships
(INFER) Army JAG/lawyer background = minimum legal credential
weaknesses: (INFER) No DOJ or prosecutorial experience
(INFER) Would require cabinet reshuffle and second confirmation fight at EPA
(INFER) Not viewed as legal heavyweight by DOJ career ranks
(INFER) Limited national security / criminal division depth
obstacles: (INFER) Double-confirmation burden (new AG + EPA successor) strains floor time
(INFER) Democrats attack his 2020 election objection vote
(INFER) Faction competition with lawyer-credentialed contenders
(INFER) Optics of moving a non-lawyer-prosecutor into AG role

candidates.name: Jeanine Pirro
strengths: (INFER) Extreme Trump loyalty + long media advocacy
(INFER) Actual prosecutorial experience as former Westchester DA and judge
(INFER) Currently interim US Attorney DC — present DOJ footing
(INFER) Strong MAGA base enthusiasm and Fox-amplified support
weaknesses: (INFER) Long incendiary TV-commentary record creates confirmation landmines
(INFER) Past 2020/Dominion claims invite litigation-style hearings
(INFER) Limited federal DOJ management experience at scale
(INFER) Polarizing even among Senate Republicans
obstacles: (INFER) Likely loses Collins, Murkowski on thin margin
(INFER) Defamation-adjacent history weaponized in hearings
(INFER) Fox-host → AG pipeline repeats Hegseth-style fight
(INFER) Age and succession-planning questions

candidates.name: Mike Davis
strengths: (INFER) Maximal MAGA-base enthusiasm + Article III Project brand
(INFER) Former Grassley chief counsel for nominations — knows Senate Judiciary cold
(INFER) Total Trump loyalty signaled publicly and repeatedly
(INFER) Would energize populist faction
weaknesses: (INFER) Provocative public statements alienate moderates
(INFER) No prosecutorial or DOJ line experience
(INFER) Never held Senate-confirmed executive post
(INFER) Viewed by institutionalists as too combative for AG
obstacles: (INFER) Highly likely to lose multiple GOP senators on temperament grounds
(INFER) Hearings would surface inflammatory social-media record
(INFER) No natural Senate champion to whip votes
(INFER) Competing against candidates with cleaner confirmation paths

candidates.name: John Ratcliffe
strengths: (INFER) Already twice Senate-confirmed (DNI, CIA Director) — proven path
(INFER) Former federal prosecutor (EDTX US Attorney) with real DOJ background
(INFER) Trusted Trump loyalist across both terms
(INFER) Strong standing with Senate Intel and Judiciary Republicans
weaknesses: (INFER) Would require vacating CIA, triggering another confirmation cycle
(INFER) IC profile less natural fit than criminal-law leaders
(INFER) Less visible MAGA-base passion than Pirro/Davis
(INFER) May prefer to stay at CIA, limiting willingness
obstacles: (INFER) Cabinet reshuffle cost + CIA succession fight
(INFER) Democrats revisit 2020 DNI declassification controversies
(INFER) Competing internal candidates with stronger personal-lawyer ties to Trump
(INFER) Timing/sequencing if vacancy is sudden

skeptic.favoriteWrongReason: Blanche is already serving as Acting AG with no permanent nominee submitted 7 weeks after Bondi's firing — if Trump wanted him in the chair he would have been nominated already. The delay signals the WH is shopping alternatives, and Blanche's recusal/independence baggage plus the Tillis Jan-6 litmus test make Senate math harder than 44¢ implies.
skeptic.underpricedReason: Harmeet Dhillon at an implied low-single-digit price (publicly floated, MAGA-aligned, RNC legal background, no EPA-style double-confirm burden like Zeldin and fewer on-camera landmines than Pirro) is the cleanest compromise pick if Blanche stalls — her tail is materially thicker than a 22-way uniform would suggest.
settlementTraps: Recess appointment that is later struck down by courts still resolves YES — traders pricing only Senate-confirmation paths underweight this.
Nominee confirmed by Senate but not sworn in before Jan 20, 2029 has undefined treatment.
Nominee dies or withdraws after confirmation vote but before taking office — undefined.
FVRA 210-day expiry (~late Oct 2026) could force a non-Blanche nominee suddenly, but market may not reprice until a name leaks.
Blanche being formally nominated does NOT resolve the market — only confirmation/recess appointment does, so a nomination bump is a fade trap.
'Acting' Blanche continuing past 210 days via statutory workaround does not count as resolution.
narrativeTraps: X rumor-tier chatter for Blanche creating false confirmation bias — no hard reporting cited.
'Loyalty pick = inevitable' heuristic ignores Trump's pattern of late-stage surprise nominees.
Zeldin price rising read as signal, but he carries a double-confirm burden — the move may be noise.
Pirro's MAGA-base narrative obscures concrete Collins/Murkowski vote-count problem.
Anchoring on the 54¢→44¢ Blanche drop as 'oversold' rather than as information that insiders see the nomination slipping.
Assuming the 22-way book's tail names are uniformly dead — FVRA pressure plus Tillis litmus could elevate a dark horse not currently on the floated list.

judgment.probabilityRange: Blanche 0.30-0.45; Zeldin 0.15-0.25; field (Pirro/Dhillon/Ratcliffe/Davis/other) 0.35-0.50 — wide bands reflect zero formal nomination yet
judgment.bestNonPriceReason: FVRA 210-day acting clock on Blanche expires ~late October 2026, forcing a permanent nomination decision before then; Tillis Jan-6 litmus narrows the GOP-confirmable pool.
judgment.biggestUncertainty: Whether Trump nominates Blanche himself or pivots to a compromise pick under FVRA pressure — no nominee submitted as of asOf date.
wouldChangeView: Formal Senate Judiciary nomination filing for any candidate

Recess-appointment timing posture from the White House
